description:
You are an edge-computing engineer preparing a staged rollout of a new firmware (v1.0.3) to selected IoT devices.  
All current, device–specific configuration files are already present under  

/home/user/iot_configs/

Each file is a simple key=value listing that always follows this exact four-line template (4 lines, the last line terminated by a newline):

device_id=<ID>  
ip=<IPV4>  
status=<online/offline>  
firmware_version=<SEMVER>  

Example:

device_id=dev00  
ip=192.168.1.100  
status=online  
firmware_version=1.0.0⏎  

(Where “⏎” denotes the final newline.)

For this exercise three concrete files already exist:

1. /home/user/iot_configs/dev01.cfg  
2. /home/user/iot_configs/dev02.cfg  
3. /home/user/iot_configs/dev03.cfg  

Your mission—using nothing but POSIX shell utilities, awk and sed—is to:

STEP 1 – Inventory  
• Create the directory /home/user/deployment/ if it does not yet exist.  
• Generate /home/user/deployment/online_devices.csv that lists ONLY devices whose status=online.  
• File format STRICTLY:  
  – The very first line is the header exactly:  
    device_id,ip,firmware_version  
  – Each subsequent line is the three comma-separated fields for one online device, **captured BEFORE any firmware updates are performed**.  
  – Lines must be sorted by the device_id alphabetically (ascending).  
  – The file must end with a single trailing newline.  

STEP 2 – Firmware bump  
• In every *.cfg file where status=online, replace the existing firmware_version value with 1.0.3, doing the change in-place (i.e. the file on disk is modified).  
• All other key/value pairs and the overall line order must remain untouched.  
• Offline devices must NOT be altered.

STEP 3 – Change log  
• Append (or create if missing) /home/user/deployment/firmware_update.log that documents every device whose firmware was changed in STEP 2.  
• Each line format EXACTLY (no spaces):  
  <device_id>:<old_version>->1.0.3  
  Example:  
  dev17:1.0.2->1.0.3  
• Lines must be sorted by device_id alphabetically (ascending).  
• The log must terminate with a single trailing newline.

When you are done, the automated grader will verify:

1. Content of /home/user/deployment/online_devices.csv matches the specification and captures the PRE-upgrade firmware versions.  
2. /home/user/iot_configs/*.cfg files for online devices now have firmware_version=1.0.3 while offline ones are untouched.  
3. /home/user/deployment/firmware_update.log is present, correctly formatted, correctly sorted, and lists the accurate old->new transitions.

IMPORTANT  
• Use awk and sed for the heavy-lifting text processing.  
• Do NOT hard-code any absolute paths in the generated files—only the paths given above.  
• Ensure every file you create ends with a newline so diff-based tests do not fail.  
• Do not delete or move any pre-existing files; modify in place or create new ones in the locations stated above.
truth:
Initial files that exist before the agent starts:

1. /home/user/iot_configs/dev01.cfg  
   Contents (exact, 4 lines, each ending with “\n”):  
   device_id=dev01\n  
   ip=192.168.1.101\n  
   status=online\n  
   firmware_version=1.0.2\n  

2. /home/user/iot_configs/dev02.cfg  
   device_id=dev02\n  
   ip=192.168.1.102\n  
   status=offline\n  
   firmware_version=1.0.2\n  

3. /home/user/iot_configs/dev03.cfg  
   device_id=dev03\n  
   ip=192.168.1.103\n  
   status=online\n  
   firmware_version=1.0.1\n  

Expected state AFTER successful task completion:

A. /home/user/deployment/online_devices.csv  
   device_id,ip,firmware_version\n  
   dev01,192.168.1.101,1.0.2\n  
   dev03,192.168.1.103,1.0.1\n  

B. /home/user/iot_configs/dev01.cfg  
   device_id=dev01\n  
   ip=192.168.1.101\n  
   status=online\n  
   firmware_version=1.0.3\n  

C. /home/user/iot_configs/dev02.cfg (unchanged)  
   device_id=dev02\n  
   ip=192.168.1.102\n  
   status=offline\n  
   firmware_version=1.0.2\n  

D. /home/user/iot_configs/dev03.cfg  
   device_id=dev03\n  
   ip=192.168.1.103\n  
   status=online\n  
   firmware_version=1.0.3\n  

E. /home/user/deployment/firmware_update.log  
   dev01:1.0.2->1.0.3\n  
   dev03:1.0.1->1.0.3\n
name: task_000000_7cfb0682
